topic: Eclipse/Chapman cluster consolidation — §8b.3 L3a collapse + §6.14 canonical-hub pointer
change_summary: EXP-462 proposes a two-patch consolidation of HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 Hole 1: P0 collapses three §8b.3 'L3a (PRED-095)' paragraphs (~1,800 words) into one ~620-word paragraph with cross-references to §4.2.3/§4.2.4/§4.2.5/§6.14; P1 inserts a one-sentence canonical-hub pointer at the top of §6.14 deferring the magnitude-band non-discrimination argument to §4.2.5. Review is pre-integration (status='ready-for-integration').
our_argument_summary: EXP-462 is a holistic-consolidation patch addressing the largest single contributor to the 39-occurrence eclipse/Chapman redundancy cluster identified by HOL-REDUNDANCY c2. The strategy is sound — convert §8b.3's three-paragraph re-derivation of §4.2.x content into cross-references — and the preserved content (four-layer adoption framing, codified Pass band, dual-Sq Fail-clause analysis, ADOPTED label) covers §8b.3's genuinely unique fair-auditor-list adjudication contribution. Concerns are execution-level, not strategic.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: EXP-462 itself does not change any dome-code claim — it consolidates prose only. The underlying claims being moved/cross-referenced rely on: (a) monitor.py line 512 'ecm_eclipse = -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672' (covered by §4.2.4); (b) inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 Ebro 'BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81' (NOT covered by any other section — see Hole #1); (c) the −10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22 'KAPPA applied twice' arithmetic (NOT covered elsewhere — see Hole #1).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: n/a — proposal is prose consolidation, not code change
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: EXP-462's claim that §6.14 audits 'internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity' over-states what §6.14 actually contains. §6.14 covers BOU vs W004 baseline selection (yes) but does NOT cover the 'KAPPA applied twice' arithmetic or the inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 Ebro detail. Those findings appear in §8b.3 alone (verified via grep across data/sections.json and data/wins.json — 'KAPPA applied twice', 'latitude_factor', 'inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 Ebro' all return only the §8b.3 L3a #2 paragraph being collapsed).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: EXP-462 is a prose-consolidation proposal, not a WIN; no monitor.py automation applies. (Tag retained for schema compliance.)
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: n/a — meta-document patch, not a dome claim

holes_found.description: P0 consolidated paragraph cross-references §6.14 for 'internal BOU/W004/monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity', but §6.14 does NOT contain the 'KAPPA applied twice' finding (−10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22 baseline with explicit 1.672 multiplier producing ~−29 nT) or the inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 Ebro calculation (BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 → ~−8 nT). Verified by grep across data/sections.json: 'KAPPA applied twice', 'latitude_factor', '−10.9 × 1.672' all appear ONLY in §8b.3 (the paragraph being collapsed). §6.14 covers BOU/W004 baseline selection and the κ-vise cross-event extrapolation, but the specific monitor.py-KAPPA-squared internal-heterogeneity audit is unique to L3a #2. If the consolidation lands as-written, these unique findings disappear from the document entirely. The 'three dome-authored formulas coexist' premise underlying the consolidated paragraph's '...is a selection from coexisting dome formulas rather than a forced derivation' claim becomes an assertion without supporting evidence visible in any cross-referenced section.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) preserve a brief ~50-word sentence in the consolidated §8b.3 paragraph naming the KAPPA-squared and inject_ai_layer.py-PRED-001 findings inline (e.g., 'The repository also contains two non-W004 ΔZ formulas — monitor.py line 512 applies KAPPA twice (−10.9 × 1.672 ≡ −18.22, then explicit 1.672 multiplier) producing ~−29 nT, and inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 computes BOU × coverage × latitude_factor 0.81 → ~−8 nT — neither matching the published W004 cluster.'); OR (b) bundle a coordinated P2 patch with EXP-462 that adds these findings to §6.14 under a new 'Internal formula heterogeneity' h3 between 'How κ was derived' and 'What κ predicts during past eclipses'; OR (c) split this off as a separate EXP and hold P0 until P2 lands. As written, P0's cross-reference does not carry its assigned load.

holes_found.description: P1 §6.14 inserted-pointer paragraph claims 'The magnitude-only Pass band non-discrimination argument — that (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) is shared arithmetic between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment — lives at §4.2.5'. But §4.2.5 does NOT contain that argument. §4.2.5 covers three structural problems (no dome geometry enters the formula, Kp<2 quiet-condition filtering, third-attempt with widening criteria). The magnitude-band non-discrimination argument as stated in the pointer has no canonical home in §4.2.x — verified by grep on 'baseline × coverage × geometry', 'shared arithmetic', 'Chapman-Sq-dynamo' across data/sections.json: matches only in §2b (the stratification paragraph, which itself defers to §4.2.4/§4.2.5), in §8b.3 (the paragraph being collapsed), and in §9.2. The §2b paragraph already commits the same cross-reference error EXP-462 is propagating — saying '§4.2.4 and §4.2.5' is canonical when it isn't quite. P1 inherits that pre-existing pointer-mismatch and amplifies it by making §6.14 also defer to a section that doesn't carry the load. Net effect: a reader who clicks the §4.2.5 link from either P0 or P1 will not find the argument it was promised; the argument has been removed from §8b.3 and is not in §4.2.5.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) re-target the P1 pointer to '§2b.6 (the stratification paragraph) or §8b.3's own preceding paragraphs' where the argument is actually written; OR (b) coordinate a P2 patch that adds a paragraph to §4.2.5 making the magnitude-band non-discrimination argument explicit (using the L3a #2 wording: 'Any coverage-scaled ionospheric-conductivity reduction model calibrated on past eclipse-magnetic data will produce signals in the same magnitude band — the arithmetic is (baseline × coverage × geometry weight), and both the dome's W004 recalibration and standard Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatments apply that arithmetic to the same observational corpus (Kim & Chang 2018 39-eclipse ensemble + BOU 2017).'); OR (c) accept the §2b pre-existing pointer convention and re-word P1 to 'lives at §2b.6 with arithmetic detail at §4.2.4 (FSF tautology) and structural framing at §4.2.5'. Option (b) is the cleanest because it lands the argument in its named canonical home and resolves the §2b pre-existing pointer-mismatch in the same operation.

holes_found.description: P0 removes the defender-dialogic structure from L3a #3 — the '(A dome defender may reply that monitor.py is internal tooling not a public commitment; we accept that scope-limit and narrow the critique accordingly — the concern is formula-selection among coexisting dome formulas, not the public commitment itself.)' passage. The narrowed conclusion ('the published W004 cluster is a selection from coexisting dome formulas') IS preserved in the consolidation. But the dialogic structure is part of what §8b.3 uniquely contributes — the EXP's own kernel_of_truth field says §8b.3's value is 'adjudicating the dome author's own fair-auditor checklist on its own terms, point-by-point.' Removing the visible 'defender objection → we narrow' move leaves only the narrowed conclusion, which reads as if we always held the narrow scope; a fair-auditor reader cannot see that we accepted a scope-limit.
holes_found.recommendation: Restore as a single sentence in the consolidated paragraph, just before 'Two issues survive consolidation...': 'We accept the dome-defender scope-limit that monitor.py is internal tooling not a public commitment, narrowing the critique to formula-selection among coexisting public-facing dome formulas.' Adds ~30 words and preserves the fair-auditor adjudication character. Low-stakes restore; consolidation can land without it if the integrator prefers the tighter text.

holes_found.description: P0 drops the inline link to PRED-095's detail_reasoning cherry-picking quote (the original L3a #3 had 'PRED-095 detail reasoning records the same cherry-picking concern: monitor.py's −29 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the W004 window, so the published range "may be cherry-picked from favorable historical events."'). The substance is preserved in data/predictions.json:PRED-095.detail_reasoning (verified — the field reads 'the dome's monitor.py already showed a −29.1 nT value for a prior eclipse that fell outside this window — the range may be cherry-picked from favorable historical events'), so the cross-reference can be restored without authoring new prose. Without it, §8b.3 readers lose the in-page pointer to the dome author's own admission of potential cherry-picking — which is rhetorically strong because it lets the dome author speak against the dome's claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Restore as parenthetical in the consolidated paragraph, attached to the 'selection from coexisting dome formulas' clause: '(the dome author's own PRED-095 detail reasoning notes monitor.py's −29.1 nT for a prior eclipse falls outside the published W004 window, conceding the range may be cherry-picked from favorable historical events).' Or simpler: '(see PRED-095 detail reasoning for the dome author's own potential-cherry-picking admission).' Either form preserves the inline link. Low-stakes restore.

holes_found.description: P0 consolidation drops the 'with FSF from V12 H(r)/r' qualifier from the ΔZ formula citation. Original L3a #1: '(ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, with FSF from V12 H(r)/r)'. Consolidation: '(ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF)'. The V12 H(r)/r dome-version provenance is mildly useful — it grounds the formula in a specific dome model version rather than a generic abstraction — but the FSF = tan(lat) tautology that depends on the H(r) shape is presented in §4.2.4 (E-PRED-B subsection), so the cross-reference covers the load. Cosmetic loss.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: change '(ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF)' to '(ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF, with FSF derived from V12 H(r)/r dome geometry)' in the consolidated paragraph. ~6 words. No-op for the argument.

holes_found.description: P0 consolidation tightens 'BOU 2017 (per-station ~−10 nT) and W004 empirical (per-station ~−20 nT, clustered at −17 to −21 nT for stations above 80% coverage)' to 'BOU 2017 per-station ~−10 nT, W004 empirical per-station ~−20 nT'. The dropped sub-clause specifies that the W004 per-station band is itself anchored to the codified Pass band (−17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage). The codified Pass band is restated in the consolidated paragraph's earlier 'observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations...' definition, so the load is carried two clauses away. Not a real loss.
holes_found.recommendation: No change required. Codified Pass band restatement earlier in the same paragraph covers this.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'three dome-authored formulas coexist' framing is strong but currently asymmetric. The consolidation says the published W004 cluster is a selection from 'coexisting dome formulas' — but the document only names three (BOU, W004, monitor.py KAPPA-squared) AND the consolidation removes the monitor.py-KAPPA-squared content. After consolidation, the reader sees 'selection from coexisting formulas' with two visible formulas (BOU, W004) and a defunct cross-reference for the third. Restoring the three-formula naming inline (per Hole #1 Recommendation (a)) tightens this asymmetry and makes the 'selection not derivation' charge concrete.

EXP-462's anticipated_objections[1] notes that the integrator may split the consolidated paragraph at the 'Two issues survive consolidation...' clause for readability. Stronger: the integrator should also consider extracting the dual-Sq-reading Fail-clause analysis (interpretations A and B) into a separate

...

with a transitional phrase like '

The Fail-clause we appended to the Pass band admits two natural readings...'. This produces three paragraphs (factual setup, two-issue summary, Fail-clause analysis) instead of one mega-paragraph, restoring §8b.3 readability without losing any content.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-462's diagnosis is correct and its scoping is honest. §8b.3's three L3a paragraphs do substantially re-derive arithmetic already presented in §4.2.4 (the monitor.py hardcoded literals, the E-PRED enumeration, the WIN-014 E-PRED-C inversion link — all confirmed present at data/sections.json:part4 anchor 'p4-424'), in §4.2.5 (the structural 'no dome geometry enters' argument at anchor 'p4-425'), and in §4.2.3 (the dual-baseline hedging at anchor 'p4-423'). The consolidation strategy (collapse three paragraphs of re-derivation into one paragraph with cross-references) is the right tool. The honest accounting in quantitative_verification (~1,200 words recovered vs c2's ~3,000 estimate) and the explicit deferral of Hole 8 (0 nT strawman) and the §5.0 Kill-Shot 5 sweep to coordinated follow-up EXPs is exactly the granularity discipline holistic-consolidation patches require. The preserved content (four-layer L1/L2/L3a/L3b/L4 adoption framing, codified Pass band, (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) checklist scaffold, dual-Sq-reading Fail-clause analysis, ADOPTED label, OPEN-001 admission, WIN-025 globe-fingerprint link — verified by reading the proposed replacement_html_for_l3a_block field) covers §8b.3's genuinely unique fair-auditor-list-adjudication contribution.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The strategy is right; the execution-level cross-reference targeting is wrong in two load-bearing places. P0's '§6.14 audits monitor.py-KAPPA-squared heterogeneity' and P1's '§4.2.5 holds the magnitude-band non-discrimination argument' are both factually incorrect about the destination sections' content (verified by grep). The fix is small (~50-100 words of inline restoration in §8b.3 OR a coordinated P2 patch to §6.14) but not optional — without it, two unique pieces of content (KAPPA-squared finding, inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 detail) disappear from the document, and one load-bearing argument (magnitude-band non-discrimination) ends up with a circular cross-reference. The kernel-of-truth claim — 'consolidation is right in principle' — holds; the no-loss claim in the EXP's anticipated_objections[0] (asserting 'every empirical binding' is preserved) does not hold for the two findings identified in Hole #1.

text_fingerprint.verdict: n/a — EXP proposal review, no WIN verdict
text_fingerprint.notes: Fingerprint reflects the EXP-462 proposal file size (28828 chars). EXP target §8b.3 current size pre-P0: 12907 chars (verified by extracting <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers"...</details> block from data/sections.json:part8b). §6.14 current size pre-P1: 23067 chars (anchor 'p6-614-kappa-cluster').

deeper_analysis:
Three structural observations that go beyond per-hole findings:

(1) The §2b stratification paragraph already commits the same cross-reference mismatch this EXP propagates. §2b says: 'The arithmetic argument is canonical to §4.2.4 and §4.2.5'. But §4.2.4 covers per-sub-test non-discrimination (E-PRED-A/D non-discriminating, E-PRED-B FSF=tan(lat) tautology) and §4.2.5 covers structural problems (no dome geometry enters, Kp filtering, third-attempt pattern) — neither makes the 'shared arithmetic (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) between dome W004 and Chapman-Sq-dynamo' argument in canonical form. The argument as such currently lives only in §8b.3's L3a #2 (being removed) and §9.2 (per the §2b pointer language). Recommendation: a follow-up EXP that adds the explicit magnitude-band non-discrimination paragraph to §4.2.5, after which §2b's existing pointer and P1's new pointer both land correctly. This is a meta-level cross-reference-graph integrity issue and should be tracked separately from EXP-462's integration.

(2) The 'three dome-authored formulas' framing strengthens after consolidation if the KAPPA-squared and inject_ai_layer.py findings are retained inline. Currently, the consolidation claims 'selection from coexisting dome formulas' but visible after-consolidation evidence shows only BOU and W004. The third formula (monitor.py KAPPA-squared) is the one that lets the 'selection not forced derivation' charge bite — without it visible somewhere in §8b.3 or §6.14, the charge is asserted, not earned. The 50-word inline restore in Hole #1 Recommendation (a) is the minimum surface-area fix; the coordinated §6.14 expansion in Recommendation (b) is structurally cleaner because it puts the KAPPA-squared analysis in the κ-cluster section where it belongs.

(3) The EXP's anticipated_objections[2] notes a low risk that §4.2.x will be renumbered. This is correct for §4.2.3/§4.2.4/§4.2.5 (stable since V6 restructure). But §6.14's 'p6-614-kappa-cluster' anchor depends on §6 not being renumbered, and §6 is a large section that may attract restructuring in a future holistic pass. The P1 insertion adds another link-target dependency to §6.14 (the §4.2.5 cross-reference from the inserted paragraph). Risk is low but not zero; the integrity agent should watch the §6.14 anchor on subsequent holistic-restructure passes.

summary_for_decider: MINOR EDIT recommended before integration. EXP-462's strategy (collapse three §8b.3 L3a re-derivations into one paragraph with cross-references) is sound and the preserved content correctly covers §8b.3's unique fair-auditor-list contribution. Two major execution-level holes: (1) P0 cross-references §6.14 for 'monitor.py-KAPPA-squared formula heterogeneity' but §6.14 does NOT contain the 'KAPPA applied twice' or inject_ai_layer.py PRED-001 findings — they appear only in §8b.3 (verified via grep across data/sections.json); after consolidation those unique findings disappear from the document. (2) P1 §6.14 pointer says the magnitude-band non-discrimination argument 'lives at §4.2.5' but §4.2.5 covers structural problems differently; the argument has no canonical home in §4.2.x and currently lives only in §8b.3 L3a #2 (being removed) and §2b briefly. Recommended fix: either (a) restore ~50 words inline in P0's consolidated paragraph naming the KAPPA-squared and inject_ai_layer.py findings, AND re-target P1 to §2b.6 (or add to §4.2.5 first); or (b) coordinate a P2 patch adding the KAPPA-squared analysis to §6.14 and the magnitude-band argument to §4.2.5, then land P0+P1+P2 together. Two moderate findings: P0 removes the defender-dialogic 'we accept scope-limit' structure (~30 words to restore) and the inline link to PRED-095 cherry-picking quote (~15 words). Two minor findings: V12 H(r)/r qualifier dropped, W004 clustering sub-clause dropped (no action needed). Consolidation should NOT land as-written; once Hole #1 and Hole #2 are addressed, this is good to integrate. Confidence: 0.78. Reviewing: pre-integration of section-rewrite proposal, no actual integrated content yet. Tests will pass either way (no numeric claims change).
